phone_intake_capture.summary_for_staff: Complex family estate / guardianship dispute involving Daniel Whitfield, a disputed property or trust issue, and multiple family members.

transcript.speaker: Voice Layer
transcript.text: Thanks for calling GuideHerd Legal Intake. I can help capture the basics. What is your name and what is this about?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: I'm Sarah Whitfield. I'm calling about my dad, Daniel Whitfield. He died last Sunday, and now there is a mess with his house, his trust, and my stepmother.

transcript.speaker: Voice Layer
transcript.text: I understand. Is there already a probate case or any court hearing scheduled?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Not that I know of. My stepmother, Lena Whitfield, keeps saying she can handle it, but my cousin Marcus Reed told me she is trying to move money out of a joint account and list the cabin in Limestone County without talking to the rest of us.

transcript.speaker: Voice Layer
transcript.text: Are there any deadlines, emergency hearings, or other family issues we should flag?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Yes. My mom, Ruth Whitfield, has an emergency guardianship hearing next Tuesday because her dementia has gotten worse. I think there may be a trust issue tied to the deed too. I have a photo of the deed and the account statements, but I don't know what matters most.

transcript.speaker: Voice Layer
transcript.text: That is helpful. Do you know the county or court handling the guardianship hearing, and has anyone already filed anything?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: I only know it is in Limestone County. Nobody has given me a case number yet. I just need someone to tell me who can stop the house sale and whether the trust is valid.

internal_warnings: Conflict names extracted only; no conflict decision made
